statement.markdown: Given a list of items, calculate the direction of a flexbox container based on its children's widths. If the sum of all children's widths is larger than the parent's width, set the direction to 'row-reverse'. Otherwise, set the direction to 'row'.
statement.inputFormat: A string array where each string represents the width of an item, separated by spaces.
statement.outputFormat: A string representing the direction of the flexbox container.
statement.notes: The widths are in the format 'xx%', where xx is an integer between 1 and 99, inclusive. The parent's width is 100%.

examples.explanation: The sum of the widths is 100%, so the direction is 'row'.
The sum of the widths is 100%, and the largest width is 80%, so the direction is 'row-reverse'.

hints.text: Start by summing up the widths of all items

editorial.title: Flexbox Direction Calculation
editorial.intuition: To determine the direction, calculate the sum of the widths and compare it to the parent's width.
editorial.bruteForce: Iterate through the widths, sum them up, and compare the result to the parent's width.
editorial.betterSolution: Use a single loop to sum up the widths and compare the result to the parent's width.
editorial.optimalSolution: Use a single line of code to sum up the widths and compare the result to the parent's width.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: The direction is 'row' if the sum of the widths is less than or equal to the parent's width, and 'row-reverse' otherwise.
commonMistakes: Forgetting to handle the case where the sum of the widths is equal to the parent's width.
